question: Has the frozen waterfall (ice cascade) been transformed into flowing liquid water?
Has the frozen river surface been converted into liquid water?
Do the trees display green foliage appropriate for summer instead of snow-covered branches?
Are the heavy layers of snow removed from the rocks and ground?
Are the hanging icicles on the cliff faces removed or replaced with flowing water?
Has the overall color palette shifted from cold blues/whites to warmer earth tones and greens?
Does the waterfall show realistic characteristics of flowing water (e.g., motion blur, mist, cascading streams) rather than solid ice texture?
Does the river/pool surface exhibit liquid properties (ripples, reflections) rather than a cracked ice texture?
Do the exposed rock surfaces show natural stone colors (brown/grey) rather than blue ice tints?
Is the structural shape of the canyon walls and cliffs preserved?
Is the large boulder in the foreground (left side) preserved in position and shape?
Are the positions and layout of the trees consistent with the original image?
Is the edited image free of unnatural artifacts (smudging, floating pixels) where ice/snow was removed?
Are the transitions between water, rocks, and vegetation seamless and visually natural?
